# Course Overview

GRA 4150 Machine Learning for Finance is a graduate-level course that builds
applied machine learning skills from first principles through deep learning.
The course emphasizes both mathematical understanding and practical
implementation in Python, with applications drawn from finance and business.

This is an English-only course in LearnForge. Canonical content lives in
reusable LearnForge objects.

## Knowledge Goals

- Understand the three machine learning paradigms: supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning
- Explain linear regression, gradient descent, and regularization techniques
- Describe the progression from linear classifiers (Adaline, logistic regression) to multi-layer perceptrons
- Understand the bias-variance tradeoff and its implications for model selection
- Explain convolutional neural network architectures and their components
- Identify sources of bias in AI systems and articulate fairness-accuracy tradeoffs

## Skills Goals

- Implement regression and classification models using scikit-learn
- Build and train neural networks using PyTorch
- Preprocess data with appropriate scaling, encoding, and splitting strategies
- Evaluate models using cross-validation, learning curves, and appropriate metrics
- Analyze fairness properties of classification systems

## General Competence

- Critically assess when machine learning is appropriate for a given problem
- Communicate model choices and limitations to non-technical stakeholders
- Apply ethical reasoning to AI system design and deployment

## Planned Content Areas

- AI history, applications, and paradigm overview
- Data preprocessing and feature engineering
- Linear regression, polynomial regression, and regularization
- Gradient descent optimization
- Adaline, perceptron, and threshold classifiers
- Logistic regression and multi-class classification
- Multi-layer perceptrons and backpropagation
- AI ethics, bias, fairness, and explainability (SHAP, XAI)
- Convolutional neural networks and image classification

## Teaching Format

Weekly lectures combining theory presentation with live coding demonstrations.
Two structured exercise sets cover mathematical foundations and practical
implementation. A three-hour digital exam assesses both conceptual understanding
and applied problem-solving.

## Tools and Workflow

- Python is the primary programming language
- NumPy, Pandas, and Matplotlib for data handling and visualization
- scikit-learn for classical ML models and preprocessing
- PyTorch for neural network implementation
- Course assets are maintained as reusable objects in LearnForge's object model
